text: Today we will talk about how to fetch data in Next.js There are 3 ways to fetch data in Next.js 1. SSR which is server side rendering 2. SSG which is static side generation 3. ISR which is incremental static regeneration We will learn all this in detail in this video But don't forget to like this video and write Next.js op in the comment section below Let's go to the computer screen Let's roll the intro So guys this is my webdub folder in which I will create a folder named video133 And I will create a new Next.js app I will open the terminal by right clicking And in the terminal I will create the same command Next app latest And I will name this app SSR ISG ISR I will tell you what is it now Type script no ESLint yes Tailwind CSS yes SRC no App router yes And we will not change the import alias Now let's talk about what is it So I will open this Next.js app and show you So here I will open it like npm run dev And see here this is a very basic app This is the same app that Next.js always gives us Now I will tell you 3 concepts The first concept is server side rendering Which we also call SSR What is this server side rendering So see in Next.js Suppose this is our component Which is our homepage component I will remove it here I will write div and I will write IAM homepage And if I show you now So this is how IAM homepage is visible Now in this IAM homepage Whatever I do in this page That is rendered on the server side Because I have not put the use client here So by default any component In Next.js is a server component So whatever I do here will be on the server If I write here Console.log hello And I run this app So I show you this hello is coming here Because it was reloaded And because of its reloading This hello is being printed here So this is being printed on the server side This is our server side And this console is our client side So if I want to print this hello here So I have to write use client first By coming here So that this client will become a component So what I will do is I will edit it in its readme It will look a little better So I will write about SSR here I will write SSR here So what is SSR SSR is server side rendering So I close it and I write it well SSR is server side rendering Server side rendering means Things are being rendered on the server And this is default behavior So I write here this is default behavior After this, the second thing is SSG And this is static site generation Now what is this SSG Look server side rendering is by default Now you will do any fetch here You will hit any request here The request that will go to you It will go through the server side It will not go through the client side Because this is your server component We understood this Now let's talk about SSG Which is static site generation Now how does this happen Now see what used to happen These terms are generation These terms were popular When pages router was very popular Next year there was no app router All things were done with pages router And what used to happen in this SSR was done By using get static props type functions But this kind of functions Don't work after app router So this app router gets Server side props function That will not work for you Yes in next year's version 14 or 15 You can do it by using pages router Now let's talk about static site generation What is static site generation Static site generation means When you build any page So it will be built statically Or it will be a part of the server side What does it mean Once you develop your app So what happens Once you develop your app So what happens is Your app will be built Before deploying And in this way you build next npm run Which internally runs next build See it is running next build And it is making an optimized production build So you see here It is making optimized production build It is taking time It is compiled successfully Now see it is generating static pages It generated 5 static pages It generated static pages from coin1 It generated not found page It generated slash page This is static page Pre rendered as static content Why? To optimize the delivery of pages Next year's app developer Decided that there is no such thing in this page Which i can run on server side Why not i build it beforehand So that it doesn't need to be built again and again Because if there was any network call Because there was any network call Because of which it would be needed to be built again and again Because of which it would be needed to be built again and again So it would make sense to build it So it is pre rendered as static content So in your production build In that This page will be shown to you as static content And this is our static site generation So any content Which does not have Network calls Is a static page By default So this is also done by default Now we talk about Incremental site Generation Is called ISG Or incremental static regeneration Is called incremental static regeneration Is called incremental static regeneration What is this? Now understand the point There is going to be a twist in the story We have seen what server side rendering is It runs on the server It runs on the server Static site generation If there is no call, then the static page will be rendered When you build Now assume you want your page To change every 60 seconds So what happened in nextjs Nextjs fetch function I will show you How is the data fetching of nextjs In nextjs Now see if you use fetch In nextjs This is not a normal fetch function Which we use in javascript Fetch in nextjs Has been modified a little What does it mean? It has a very simple meaning That in nextjs You have put a fetch call and you will put the same call again Like they have given an example It is a very nice example, understand it I am copying it and pasting it here I paste it here In fact here And see what we are doing here First of all we are making this function an async function We are saying Go to api.versal.app.block Which looks like this In the API of Versal They have given a sample data And Now see here We have taken the post And then we have mapped it And this is a very simple thing All the posts will be visible here I think I have closed the site I run npm run dev And I will reload it again So all our blogs Will be visible From this data Everything is fetched here Now if I refresh it It will be very fast And the reason for that is This network call Will not go again and again Why? Because it has gone once Now it will be cached The cache will be there And if it changes later It will be like this You will see it like this And some people think it is a problem In Next.js They say why is it not working You know what is my reason The reason is very simple Next.js by default caches Now you want new data every time So what you have to do That here in Next.js You have to do no store In this way Cache and no store I will copy it You will simply Do this You are telling it That fetch api Run it again and again This data is going to change By default it will not run For your benefit So that your network calls are saved Your server compute calls are saved Your app is optimized So api.versal.app Will get request again and again And it is written here If you don't want to catch the response from fetch You can do the following Now let's talk about ISR ISR is a very simple thing What you have to do You want incrementally With time Revalidate the page So once the page is cached Now you want it to be revalidated with time So you will simply do this And now what will happen It will take the best of both words Sometimes you update your blog And you want To get updates It should not be that You don't get updates It should not be that you get updates every time And the whole data is fetched from the base You don't want that What you will do You will give this option Next revalidate 3600 seconds So this revalidate Is a number Which is number of seconds This is 3600 seconds This is 60 minutes So in every hour your page will be revalidated And after revalidation You will get an updated page Very good Now there are two exceptions here Where fetch request is not cached The first exception is Whenever you use fetch in server action And whenever you use it in route handler So I will write this I will write this here Fetch in next.js Caches The response However there are exceptions And those exceptions are these Those exceptions are these What are these? These are So I have written exceptions here Whenever you use it in server action Or in route handler In post method It is not cached Now I will show you one thing I told you that page is pre-rendered If I do something like this on this page I will do something like this First let's see what happens by default I will build next I will build npm run So you will see This page will be built as a static page I will show you It is built as a static page If I Do something like this Now see it has built it as a static page Which means This response will not change again and again It will be revalidated every one hour But if I add here export const dynamic is equal to force dynamic Then it will not be rendered as a static page Then it will not be rendered as a static page Let's wait for it And let's see this in action And see here It is rendered as a dynamic page See here the slash And it is saying that it is dynamic It is server rendered on demand Why? Because you told it You said that my page will be server rendered I don't want to play this game here So what can you do by writing this You can tell by writing this Don't cache my fetch request Don't cache my fetch request Show me my page by fetching it every time Show me my page by fetching it every time So which rendering is this? This is server side rendering If you want your server side rendering to be made If you want your server side rendering to be made By default it is server side rendering But if you are using fetch Then you have to use cache And if you want to opt out of that cache then use this And I will write here To opt out To opt out Use And you can use this code You can use this code Now I will copy and paste it So that it becomes easy for you And you see here Export const dynamic Is equal to force dynamic Let's also put semicolon However there are exceptions I have already told you So this is our server side rendering Static side generation ISR and ISG Incremental static generation Or incremental static regeneration It's the same thing Now here How data is fetched How and when it works You have to read it fully to know its caveats But let me tell you What I told you people that was 95% And whatever you learn You can't learn more than 95% Because It is documentation 100% of information You can go to the documentation You can read it, you will understand It's not that you won't understand Try it You can read this part If you want to master this thing Otherwise what I told you people Use this If your page is not being re-rendered You need to use this If you want to fetch new data You need to use this I hope you liked this video And you understood What is SSR, SSG and ISG Thank you so much for watching this video And I will see you Next time Outro
